layout.name: MarshallFire
layout.description: Public marketing site for the Marshall Fire mass-tort intake.

brand.name: Marshall Fire Claims
brand.tagline: Justice for Boulder County fire survivors

hero.title: If you lost your home in the Marshall Fire, we can help.
hero.subtitle: Free case review for Superior, Louisville, and unincorporated Boulder County residents affected by the December 30, 2021 fire.

footer.text: © 2026 Marshall Fire Claims Group — Not legal advice. No attorney-client relationship formed by website use.

pages.name: Home
pages.description: We represent homeowners, renters, and business owners affected by the Marshall Fire. If your property was damaged or destroyed in Superior, Louisville, or unincorporated Boulder County on December 30, 2021, you may be entitled to compensation.

blocks.name: WhoWeHelp
blocks.description: The Marshall Fire destroyed over 1,000 homes and damaged hundreds more. Our team is reviewing cases involving real property loss, personal property loss, business interruption, personal injury, and evacuation damages.

pages.name: Eligibility
pages.description: Who qualifies for the Marshall Fire mass-tort claim.

blocks.name: EligibilityCriteria
blocks.description: Qualifying criteria: (1) property located in the affected zones — Superior, Louisville, or unincorporated Boulder County; (2) damage or loss sustained on December 30, 2021; (3) evidence of residency or ownership; (4) filed within the statute of limitations.

pages.name: Intake
pages.description: Start your free case review. Your submission is confidential and there is no obligation.

blocks.name: SubmitIntake
blocks.description: Tell us about your property and what was lost. An attorney will review your submission within two business days.

fields.name: address
fields.label: Street address of the affected property

fields.name: description
fields.label: Tell us what happened and what was lost

pages.name: FAQ
pages.description: Common questions about the Marshall Fire litigation.

blocks.name: FaqList
blocks.description: Q: Is there a cost to get a case review? A: No. The review is free and there is no obligation.  Q: What documents should I gather? A: Insurance policies, photos of damage, receipts, and proof of residency.  Q: How long do I have to file? A: Statutes of limitations apply — contact us as soon as possible for an assessment.
